mission.title: Install and Configure Sermant on Kubernetes
mission.description: Sermant is a proxyless service mesh based on Java bytecode enhancement technology, designed to provide service governance capabilities in microservices architectures. Installing Sermant allows you to enhance your Java applications with governance features without modifying their source code.

steps.title: Add the Sermant Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Sermant Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add sermant https://sermant-io.github.io/Sermant
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Sermant using Helm
steps.description:
Install Sermant with the specified version and create a namespace for it:
```bash
helm install sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Sermant pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n sermant
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for Sermant by editing the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Sermant backend
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Sermant backend service to access it:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/sermant-backend 8080:80 -n sermant
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Sermant release from your cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall sermant --namespace sermant
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove any CRDs that were created during the installation:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
# List CRDs to find the names:
kubectl get crd
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Sermant namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace to clean up all resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace sermant
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Take a backup of the current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all -n sermant -o yaml > sermant-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Sermant Helm chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Sermant
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Sermant installation to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Sermant pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n sermant
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n sermant
# Look for events indicating insufficient resources or scheduling issues.
```

troubleshooting.title: Sermant backend not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Sermant backend, ensure the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n sermant
# Check if the backend service is listed and the correct ports are exposed.
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage, consider adjusting resource limits:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant
# Modify the resources section to allocate more memory or CPU.
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Sermant will have all pods running in the 'sermant' namespace, and you will be able to access the backend service through the forwarded port. You can also verify the service governance capabilities in your Java applications.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add sermant https://sermant-io.github.io/Sermant
helm repo update
helm install sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant --create-namespace

kubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and that Helm and kubectl are installed on your local machine.
